all_results.quote: general models will win out over specialized ones in most cases

all_results.label: Generative AI Utility vs Factuality

all_results.label: Hardware-Software Co-design

all_results.label: Optimize for Experimental Velocity
all_results.quote: because there's a very different qualitative feel to doing science and research in a domain where an experiment takes a month versus doing it in a domain where you know minutes or hours you get you know an answer

all_results.label: Push on Latency
all_results.quote: I'm a big believer in pushing on latency because I think being able to have really low latency interactions with a system you're using is just much more delightful than something that is, you know, 10 times as slow

all_results.label: Reasoning over Memorization

all_results.label: Scale and Algorithms Multiply

all_results.label: 1/1000th Scale Vetting

all_results.label: Back-of-the-Envelope System Design

all_results.quote: if we see a gradient spike, then we will effectively replay that step or the last few steps and see if we get the same answer. And if we get the same answer, we conclude, well, that's probably okay. That was probably due to the data, not due to hardware errors.

all_results.label: Model Distillation

all_results.quote: you essentially get indistinguishable accuracy from using the real simulator but it's three hundred thousand times faster
